notes: All three graph IDs were observed through the live LangGraph assistants API.
Implementation and review used separate graph, thread, run, and assistant identities.

lessons: A LangGraph success state is submission/runtime evidence, not VALP completion evidence.
A short adapter wait window must preserve the runtime job and resume by run ID.
Repair replay is auditable when the same thread ID and a new run/checkpoint ID are retained.
next_routing_hints: Use one graph ID per routed role and verify it during runtime preflight.
Keep reviewer graph/thread identity separate from the implementer.
Treat missing runtime model identity as a declared limitation, not inferred proof.
worked: The adapter recorded the runtime run ID before waiting.
The first successful run was blocked because expected evidence was absent.
The repaired run reused the original worker thread and passed independent review.
did_not_work: The clean main baseline did not yet generate worker control contracts; task-local control files came from the installed unmerged generator and are recorded as source drift.
context_gaps: The deterministic graph does not expose an underlying LLM model/provider identity.

rule_change_proposals.proposal: Keep LangGraph pause-window expiry non-terminal and resumable by run ID.

privacy_notes: The fixture contains anonymous graph role names, localhost URLs, generated UUIDs, and task-local refs only.
